title: Turbulent_mixing_and_NOx_formation_in_gas_turbine_combustors
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text and images:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

NO_EQUATIONS

**2. Graphs/Charts:**
*   **(a):**
    *   X-axis: Radial Distance - Inches
    *   Y-axis: MOL% (dry)
    *   Curves: CO,  Equivalent JP-5 = 100
    *   Key values: CO at inner liner at approximately 16 MOL%, goes approximately to 0 at 1.2 inches distance. Equivalent JP-5 = 100 at inner liner goes to approximately 0 at 1 inches distance
*   **(b):**
    *   X-axis: Radial Distance - Inches
    *   Y-axis (left): MOL% (dry)
    *   Y-axis (right): AREA BELOW CURVE
    *   Curves: CO2, NOx times 10 to the 4th power (NOx x 10^4)
    *   Key values: CO2 at the inner liner at approximately 0 MOL%, rises steadily to 20 MOL% at 17 inches distance and then decreasing to 15 MOL%
    NOx x 10^4 at the inner liner at approximately 10 MOL%, decreases steadily to approximately 1 MOL% at 4 inches distance

**3. Tables:**

NO_TABLES

**4. Diagrams:**

*   **Fig. 1. Combustor schematic of cross-section.**
    *   Components: Fuel Nozzle, Staging stations, Combustor Center Line, Inner Liner, Outer Liner
    *   Labels: Dimension Values in Inches: .83%, .25%,  3.75", 6.25%, 1.0%, 5.14%, 7.15%, 7.25", 3.09%, 4.13%, 4.24%  3.48
**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   X = 7.25" (axial location)
*   P = 1.3 atm (pressure)
*   TA = 785°F (temperature)
*   F/A = 0.0193 (fuel-air ratio0
